summary: Structural review of stdin-only refactor — APPROVED. readInputData() is truly 0-arg stdin-only with zero dataArg/fileArg residue. TTY guard uses strict === true (Claude Code Bash / CI isTTY=undefined correctly proceeds). Windows EAGAIN/EOF/ENOENT + errno -4095 (UV_EOF) all normalized to NO_STDIN empty-stdin. NO_STDIN error contract is uniform across all 10 write commands (mission write-spec/brief, task create/record add, evidence add, decision record, phase write, recovery write, evolution gap-assessment/rules-update), each routing through output.ts noStdinError() helper. evidence add and task record add implement identical three-way fallback: try/catch readInputData, swallow NO_STDIN when --set present, Object.assign shallow merge with inline comments documenting the semantics. Non-goals respected: event.ts --data, context.ts --data, packet.ts --file/--content all preserved in grep verification. Scope creep bounded: 11 modified + 1 new test file — all within scope.surfaces. package.json delta is minimal and justified (wires new input.test into test runner). 11 unit tests cover every required case plus BOM and whitespace bonuses.
concerns: Non-blocking: package.json was touched but not listed in contract scope.surfaces. Justified (test runner wiring) but contract compiler should include test config files in future scopes when new test files are added.

Non-blocking: self_check.known_risks correctly flags integration.js still runs against the pre-bundled plugin/bin/geas; real-Windows EAGAIN path and real-TTY guard are mock-only in this task. Task-003 rebundle must execute a live Windows smoke to close this gap.

Non-blocking: evolution.ts catches a SyntaxError branch in its fallback that readInputData() no longer throws directly (all parser errors are rewrapped with code INVALID_JSON before leaving input.ts). Dead branch; safe to simplify in a later cleanup.
Non-blocking: V8 SyntaxError regex 'position N' will silently degrade to raw parser message on future Node versions — acknowledged in self_check, tested via fallback case, acceptable.
